Patch Set 10: Code-Review+1

(3 comments)

looks good only small issues
would be +2 verified +1

Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

There are now multiple download implementations, but some parts are in some base python?
As a concept what means "http files", it does not sound anything reasonable. 

Is there a some checksum checking in download scripts?

Patch Set 10:

(1 comment)

Checksum is not a bad idea however that would require us to provide list of file with checksums. In general there is room for creating a class , with fields: name,url,checksum and  methods like download, validate etc. However this is already a huge step forward (adding retries and removing partially downloaded files in case of failure ) I believe bash download will be removed.

Patch Set 17:

> Patch Set 12: Code-Review-1
> 
> the concept of http?
> 
> and it uses same request lib as npm. Can it moved to "base"?

for offline installer http means everything that is in http_files list. Basically maven stuff plus some blueprint's imports. Anything that can be downloaded by single http request.
Checksum checking is not implemented yet. I plan to add it in future but right now I want basic functionality for downloading these files.
